query: 2025 European Junior Swimming Championships Samorin Slovakia: Russia AIN neutral athletes gold medal results day by day. Which events did Russia win gold (5 total) and on which days? Also note 2021 Rome Russia 11 golds. Was Russia's gold haul back-loaded toward later days of the meet?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Russia (AIN neutral athletes) — 2025 European Junior Swimming Championships, Šamorín

**5 golds total, distinctly back-loaded:**

| Day | Date | Event | Athlete |
|-----|------|-------|---------|
| Day 2 | Jul 2 | Boys 50m Backstroke | Georgii Iakovlev (25.04) |
| Day 3 | Jul 3 | Boys 200m IM | Mikhail Shcherbakov (1:59.04, Championship Record) |
| Day 5 | Jul 5 | Girls 800m Freestyle | Sofia Diakova |
| Day 6 | Jul 6 | Girls 400m Freestyle | Sofia Diakova |
| Day 6 | Jul 6 | Boys 400m Freestyle | Grigorii Vekovishchev |

**Answer to your core question — Yes, clearly back-loaded:**
- **0 golds on Day 1**, 0 on Day 4 (shut out on two days)
- **3 of 5 golds (60%) came on the final two days**, and **2 of 5 (40%) on the very last day (Day 6)** — driven largely by the later-scheduled distance-freestyle finals (800m/400m)

**2021 Rome context:** Russia (then under its own flag) topped the overall medal table for the **9th consecutive year** and won the **Team Trophy**, with **26 total medals**. This is consistent with the ~11-gold figure you cited (my reachable sources firmly confirmed the table-topping and 26-medal total, and the breadth of golds — e.g., Klepikova alone won 6 — but the exact "11" gold digit couldn't be independently rendered from Wikipedia's medal table through the available tools).

⚠️ **One caution:** Some web summaries blended in **2026 Munich** data (a Misharina 400 Free CR, Vekovishchev 3:44.58, etc.). I excluded those; the five golds above are verified from the 2025 Šamorín day-by-day recaps.

sources.title: 2025 European Junior Championships: Day 2 Finals Live Recap
sources.snippet: GOLD – Georgii Iakovlev (AIN), 25.04 in Boys 50m Backstroke; AIN also bronze via Shcherbakov.

sources.title: 2025 European Junior Championships: Day 3 Finals Live Recap
sources.snippet: GOLD – Mikhail Shcherbakov (AIN), 1:59.04 in Boys 200m IM, Championship Record breaking Tom Dean's 1:59.17.

sources.title: 2025 European Junior Championships: Day Five Finals Live Recap
sources.snippet: Day 5 finals included the 800m free; Serafima Fokina (AIN) took bronze in 200 fly.

sources.title: Samorin 2025: GB top medals table after four golds on rain-interrupted final session
sources.snippet: Final-day (Day 6) recap; Diakova 400 Free gold and Vekovishchev Boys 400 Free gold for AIN.

sources.title: Great Britain Tops Final Medal Table At 2025 European Junior Championships
sources.snippet: GBR amassed 19 medals, including 9 golds, 5 silvers and 5 bronze; Italy 2nd with 4 golds.

sources.title: 2025 European Junior Championships: Day Four Finals Live Recap
sources.snippet: Day 4 golds went to Juška (100 fly, 50 free) and Nowacki (200 breast EJR); no AIN gold.

sources.title: Turkey & Poland Make Major Moves To Shake Up Euro Juniors Final Medal Table
sources.snippet: Russia topped the medal table for the 9th year in a row in 2021, claiming 26 medals (down from 36 in 2019).

sources.title: European Junior Swimming Championships, Rome — Day 6 Summary
sources.snippet: Russia topped the medal charts and won the Team Trophy; Klepikova had 6 golds; Tatarinova won 50 free plus relays.

sources.title: 2021 European Junior Swimming Championships - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: Held 6–11 July 2021 in Rome, Italy at Stadio Olimpico del Nuoto; organized by LEN.

sources.title: Daria Tatarinova - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: At 2021 EJSC won gold in 50m free (European junior record) plus three relay golds and 100m free silver.
